mechanism_narrative: Sustained job creation, as measured by non-farm payrolls, indicates a healthy and expanding economy, boosting consumer confidence and corporate earnings expectations, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If PAYEMS shows consistent month-over-month growth for 6 consecutive months, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative or flat on average during this period.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) rises significantly above a moderate level, it signals potential central bank tightening, leading to higher discount rates and negative S&P 500 forward returns as future earnings are devalued.
failure_condition: If CPIAUCSL consistently exceeds the trigger for two consecutive quarters, but S&P 500 60-day returns remain positive or flat for 6 consecutive quarters.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) combined with strong housing starts (HOUST) indicate broad-based consumer and investment demand, signaling a strong economic expansion that supports positive S&P 500 forward returns due to increased corporate revenues.
failure_condition: If both RSAFS and HOUST triggers are met, but S&P 500 20-day returns are negative in more than 50% of such instances over a 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A very low unemployment rate (UNRATE) in a late-cycle expansion indicates tight labor markets, which can lead to wage inflation and margin compression for corporations, ultimately driving negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE consistently stays below 4.0% during a period identified as a late-cycle expansion, but S&P 500 60-day returns are predominantly positive.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production (INDPRO) reflects healthy manufacturing and supply-side capacity utilization, which translates into strong corporate earnings and positive S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If INDPRO shows consistent month-over-month growth for 6 consecutive months, but S&P 500 5-day returns are negative on average during this period.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) indicates aggressive monetary tightening by the central bank, which raises borrowing costs and reduces liquidity, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS increases by > 0.25% in a single month
failure_condition: If FEDFUNDS increases by more than 0.25% in a month, but S&P 500 20-day returns are positive in over 70% of such instances over a 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: High GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with low and stable inflation (CPIAUCSL) creates an optimal 'goldilocks' scenario for corporate profits and investor sentiment, driving strong positive S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth > 2.5% AND CPIAUCSL 12-month change < 2.0%
failure_condition: If the 'goldilocks' trigger conditions are met, but S&P 500 60-day returns are negative or flat in more than 40% of occurrences over a 10-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A rapidly rising unemployment rate (UNRATE) during an economic downturn signals deteriorating labor market conditions, which further dampens consumer spending and corporate outlook, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: If UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months during a declared recession, but S&P 500 20-day returns are consistently positive.
